refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding 'documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' and 'history of baselines/calibration updates.' High safety criticality demands explicit audit and change control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and operator identity; (c) all updates are logged with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is complete and auditable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, mission context hash).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain managed history of all baseline and calibration updates with change reason, approver, and rollback capability.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability query interface to link any recorded requirement back to originating worksite input and approval chain.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies deterministic algorithms (physics-based cycle time estimation, energy models, availability calculations), and outputs requirement baselines to a versioned database with full audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Performance requirement computation scattered across Vehicle telemetry processing, Teleoperation command logic, and Safety Services, each computing partial requirements and merging results.
DP-C: Manual Baseline Entry with Post-Hoc Validation – Site Manager manually enters performance targets; system validates against mission context and logs changes, but derivation is not automated.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Human-in-Loop Approval – Automated derivation engine computes targets; human (Site Manager or Safety Manager) reviews and approves before baseline is locked; all changes require explicit approval workflow.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Human-in-Loop Approval – Automated derivation engine computes performance targets from worksite mission context using physics-based and empirical models; Site Manager or Safety Manager reviews and approves derived targets before baseline is recorded; all subsequent updates require explicit change request, technical justification, and approval; full audit trail (who, what, when, why) is maintained for every baseline version and calibration update.

potential_sources: DP-D couples performance derivation logic (algorithm) to approval workflow (human decision gate). This is acceptable because safety criticality (high) justifies human oversight; the coupling is intentional and reduces risk of unvalidated requirement changes.
DP-D requires ingestion of worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets) from Site Manager. This couples to external stakeholder input but is necessary and well-defined.
DP-D requires versioned storage and audit logging. This couples to a persistent data layer but is a standard architectural dependency, not a problematic functional coupling.
coupling_check.notes: No problematic independence violations detected. The hybrid approach (automated derivation + human approval) is a deliberate design choice to balance automation efficiency with safety governance. Coupling to approval workflow is justified by high safety criticality.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) is available and well-defined at the time of performance requirement derivation. RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be invalid. Mitigation: Require Site Manager to validate mission context completeness before derivation is triggered.

ASSUMPTION: Physics-based and empirical models for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are accurate and calibrated for the NGHE vehicle platform. RISK: Model inaccuracy leads to unrealistic performance targets. Mitigation: Establish model validation and periodic recalibration process; maintain model uncertainty bounds.

ASSUMPTION: Approval authority (Site Manager or Safety Manager) has sufficient expertise to evaluate derived performance targets. RISK: Approver may accept invalid targets or reject valid ones. Mitigation: Provide decision support (e.g., confidence intervals, sensitivity analysis) to approver; require documented justification for any override.

ASSUMPTION: Audit logging infrastructure is reliable and tamper-proof. RISK: Audit trail could be corrupted or falsified, undermining traceability. Mitigation: Implement cryptographic signing of audit records; periodic audit log integrity checks.

RISK: High safety criticality means any error in performance requirement derivation could lead to unsafe operation. Mitigation: Implement independent verification of derived targets; establish safety margin buffers in derived requirements.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) derivation algorithm details (physics models, empirical correlations, safety margin calculation); (2) approval workflow (who can approve, approval SLA, escalation path); (3) audit logging schema (fields, retention, integrity checks); (4) rollback and version control procedures. These details should be captured in lower-level DPs during next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the end-to-end performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking capability. This includes: (1) orchestration of mission context ingestion from Site Manager; (2) invocation of derivation algorithms; (3) coordination of approval workflow with Safety Manager; (4) persistent storage of baselines and audit logs; (5) exposure of traceability query interface to stakeholders. The NGHE Solution module integrates with Vehicle (for telemetry and operational constraints), Teleoperation (for mission context and operator input), and Safety Services (for safety-critical approval and constraint validation).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) flows from Site Manager via Tasking interface. NGHE Solution receives mission parameters and uses them as input to performance derivation algorithms.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle telemetry (actual cycle times, energy consumption, availability metrics) flows into NGHE Solution for model calibration and baseline validation. Used to refine empirical models and detect drift from recorded baselines.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution sends derived safety margins and constraint violations to Safety Services via SafetyAlert. Safety Services returns approval/rejection and safety-critical constraints that must be incorporated into baseline. Bidirectional for approval workflow.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with remote Tele-operator and Site Manager for mission context updates and approval notifications. Also used to distribute baseline updates to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of end-to-end performance requirement derivation and baseline management. All core logic (derivation algorithms, baseline storage, audit logging, approval workflow orchestration) is contained within this module. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces to Vehicle (telemetry input), Teleoperation (mission context input), and Safety Services (approval coordination) are necessary. This is the correct architectural home.

Vehicle module is responsible for vehicle-level control and telemetry. Performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern, not a vehicle-level concern. If derivation logic were placed in Vehicle, it would be tightly coupled to vehicle firmware, making it difficult to update derivation algorithms or approval workflows without vehicle software updates. High diffusion risk: derivation logic would be scattered across vehicle and ground systems. Not recommended.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator commands and mission execution. Performance requirement derivation is orthogonal to teleoperation control logic. Placing derivation in Teleoperation would couple mission planning (derivation) to mission execution (teleoperation), violating separation of concerns. High diffusion risk: derivation would be entangled with operator interface and command processing. Not recommended.

Safety Services is responsible for safety constraint validation and safety-critical decision making. Performance requirement derivation includes safety margin calculation, which is safety-critical. However, derivation is broader than safety (includes cycle time, energy efficiency, availability). If derivation were fully contained in Safety Services, non-safety performance targets (e.g., energy efficiency) would be incorrectly classified as safety-critical. Partial containment: Safety Services should validate and approve derived safety margins, but should not own the entire derivation engine. Recommended role: Safety Services approves derived targets; NGHE Solution owns derivation logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct architectural owner of performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking. The module has high containment (95%) because: (1) derivation is a system-level concern, not vehicle-level or operator-level; (2) baseline management and audit logging are system-level infrastructure; (3) approval workflow orchestration requires coordination across multiple stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager), which is a system-level responsibility. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and minimal, indicating low diffusion risk. Safety Services plays a supporting role (approval authority for safety-critical targets) but should not own the entire derivation engine.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is not yet fully defined or has unclear boundaries, assigning this responsibility could lead to scope creep or unclear ownership. Mitigation: Ensure NGHE Solution module charter explicitly includes 'performance requirement management' as a core responsibility.

RISK: Derivation algorithms may require frequent updates based on field experience and model calibration. If NGHE Solution is a monolithic module, updates could be slow or risky. Mitigation: Design derivation engine as a pluggable component within NGHE Solution; use version control and staged rollout for algorithm updates.

RISK: Audit logging and baseline versioning require reliable persistent storage. If NGHE Solution does not have access to a robust database, audit trail could be lost. Mitigation: Ensure NGHE Solution has access to a dedicated, redundant data store with integrity checks and backup procedures.

RISK: High safety criticality means any bug in derivation logic or approval workflow could have serious consequences. Mitigation: Implement independent verification of derived targets; establish safety margin buffers; conduct formal verification of critical algorithms; require Safety Manager approval for all baseline changes.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) Explicitly define NGHE Solution module charter to include 'performance requirement derivation and baseline management'; (2) Design derivation engine as a pluggable, versioned component to enable algorithm updates without full system rebuild; (3) Specify data persistence layer (database schema, backup, integrity checks) for baseline and audit log storage; (4) Define approval workflow in detail (approval authority, SLA, escalation path, override procedures); (5) Establish safety validation procedures (independent verification, margin buffers, formal verification of critical algorithms).

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what the system must do: derive performance requirements from worksite mission context, record baselines, and maintain managed history with audit trail. Selected DP-D (Hybrid Derivation with Human-in-Loop Approval) directly addresses all FR elements: (1) automated derivation satisfies 'derive performance requirements'; (2) baseline recording satisfies 'record'; (3) managed history with approval workflow satisfies 'manage' and 'maintain history'; (4) audit trail satisfies traceability requirement. No independence violations: derivation logic, approval workflow, and audit logging are coupled by design (intentional, justified by safety criticality). DP-D is implementable and testable. FR-DP mapping is sound.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): DP-D requires ingestion of worksite mission context from Site Manager. This is an external input dependency (via Tasking interface) but is well-defined and localized. No diffusion across multiple modules.

DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): DP-D requires approval workflow coordination with Safety Manager. This is handled via SafetyAlert interface and approval orchestration within NGHE Solution. No diffusion across multiple modules.
DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): DP-D requires telemetry input from Vehicle for model calibration. This is a read-only dependency via Telemetry interface. No diffusion of derivation logic into Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module has high containment (95%) for DP-D. All core logic (derivation algorithms, baseline storage, audit logging, approval workflow) is contained within NGHE Solution. External interfaces are minimal and well-defined (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No problematic diffusion detected. DP-IM mapping is sound.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible within NGHE Solution module. Verification approach: (1) Unit tests for derivation algorithms (verify correct computation of cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins from mission context); (2) Integration tests for baseline recording and audit logging (verify baseline is recorded with correct metadata, audit trail is complete); (3) System tests for approval workflow (verify Site Manager and Safety Manager can review and approve/reject derived targets, changes are logged); (4) Traceability tests (verify any recorded requirement can be traced back to originating worksite input and approval chain). All verification activities can be conducted within NGHE Solution module boundaries with controlled inputs (mission context, telemetry) and observable outputs (baseline records, audit logs, approval decisions).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear. NGHE Solution owns: (1) derivation algorithm implementation; (2) baseline storage and versioning; (3) audit logging; (4) approval workflow orchestration; (5) traceability query interface. Supporting roles: Vehicle provides telemetry input; Teleoperation provides mission context input; Safety Services provides approval authority and safety constraint validation. Ownership boundaries are clear and non-overlapping.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Refine DP-D to explicitly specify derivation algorithm details (physics models, empirical correlations, safety margin calculation methods). This will enable more precise verification and reduce ambiguity in implementation.

REVISION 2: Define approval workflow in detail (approval authority, approval SLA, escalation path, override procedures, documentation requirements). This will ensure consistent implementation across NGHE Solution.
REVISION 3: Specify audit logging schema (fields, retention policy, integrity checks, tamper-proofing). This will ensure audit trail is reliable and meets safety/compliance requirements.

REVISION 4: Establish data persistence layer specification (database schema, backup procedures, recovery procedures, access control). This will ensure baseline and audit log data is protected and recoverable.

REVISION 5: Define safety validation procedures (independent verification of derived targets, safety margin buffers, formal verification of critical algorithms, Safety Manager approval requirements). This will address high safety criticality.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is well-defined, DP-D is sound and implementable, and NGHE Solution is the correct architectural owner. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. However, CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic; it has been decomposed into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that require further refinement and design. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to: (1) detail derivation algorithm design (CR-NGHE-005.2); (2) specify baseline recording and metadata schema (CR-NGHE-005.3); (3) design approval workflow and change control procedures (CR-NGHE-005.4); (4) design traceability query interface (CR-NGHE-005.5). ZIGZAG_DOWN is the appropriate decision to continue functional decomposition at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirement targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance baseline with full metadata (timestamp, source, operator, mission context hash).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain managed history of all baseline and calibration updates with change reason, approver, and rollback capability.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability query interface to link any recorded requirement back to originating worksite input and approval chain.

assumptions: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) is available and well-defined at the time of performance requirement derivation.
Physics-based and empirical models for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are accurate and calibrated for the NGHE vehicle platform.
Approval authority (Site Manager or Safety Manager) has sufficient expertise to evaluate derived performance targets.
Audit logging infrastructure is reliable and tamper-proof.
NGHE Solution module has access to a robust, redundant data store for baseline and audit log storage.
Safety margin calculation methods are well-defined and can be formally verified.
open_risks: Model inaccuracy in derivation algorithms could lead to unrealistic performance targets. Mitigation: Establish model validation and periodic recalibration process; maintain model uncertainty bounds.

Approver may accept invalid targets or reject valid ones due to insufficient expertise or decision support. Mitigation: Provide decision support (e.g., confidence intervals, sensitivity analysis) to approver; require documented justification for any override.
Audit trail could be corrupted or falsified, undermining traceability. Mitigation: Implement cryptographic signing of audit records; periodic audit log integrity checks.

High safety criticality means any error in performance requirement derivation could lead to unsafe operation. Mitigation: Implement independent verification of derived targets; establish safety margin buffers; conduct formal verification of critical algorithms; require Safety Manager approval for all baseline changes.

Derivation algorithm updates could be slow or risky if NGHE Solution is a monolithic module. Mitigation: Design derivation engine as a pluggable component; use version control and staged rollout for algorithm updates.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of derivation algorithms (physics models, empirical correlations, safety margin calculation methods).
Approval workflow specification (approval authority, approval SLA, escalation path, override procedures, documentation requirements).
Audit logging schema specification (fields, retention policy, integrity checks, tamper-proofing).
Data persistence layer specification (database schema, backup procedures, recovery procedures, access control).
Safety validation procedures specification (independent verification, safety margin buffers, formal verification of critical algorithms).
Model validation and calibration procedures (how models are validated, how often they are recalibrated, how uncertainty is tracked).
Traceability query interface specification (what queries are supported, what data is returned, how traceability is maintained).
Test plan for CR-NGHE-005 (unit tests for algorithms, integration tests for baseline recording, system tests for approval workflow, traceability tests).
